items.notes: A true expression of the Marchesi de' Frescobaldi style, this wine shows impressive balance with clear aging potential. When stored properly, it should evolve beautifully over the next 8 to 12 years. Brunello, in my opinion, leans more into earthy, savoury character compared to many New World expressions, which is exactly what makes it so compelling. Here, I find layered notes of herbal spice and cherry, carried through to a long, structured finish. One of my favourite wines from an excellent producer, offering strong value for its quality.
items.pairings: Given its Italian origin, I always prefer to enjoy this wine alongside classic regional cuisine. While it is quite versatile, it truly shines with the four Roman pastas, especially Pasta all’Amatriciana. The herbal notes in the wine align beautifully with the savoury depth of the sausage, creating a seamless pairing. If you can find sausage with fennel, it elevates the experience even further. For a simpler approach, it pairs just as well with a thoughtfully built charcuterie board, particularly with a generous serving of Pecorino Romano, made from sheep’s milk and naturally saltier, which enhances the wine’s structure and brings everything into balance.
